items.title: How Paris became a nexus for Black culture
items.summary: It’s got Europe’s largest Black population, the world’s second-biggest rap scene and a long literary history. items.title: Small towns get burned by fireworks bills
items.summary: America's 250th birthday is shaping up to be a red, white and blue fireworks bonanza — for towns that can afford it. Why it matters: As cities compete for bigger, brighter anniversary displays, smaller municipalities are getting priced out — or learning there simply aren't enough crews or equipment to go around. The big picture: Blown away by the costs of competing with larger cities, some towns have resorted to choosing new dates farther from the holiday or crowdsourcing donations.
